description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 5 unit/build/flake/data cluster: build-artifact QA vs unit testing vs flake diagnosis vs data design, plus the detection-vs-remediation boundary with the shipped secrets-identity-hardener.

cases.prompt: Run the production build, check the bundle for exposed env values, and make sure the preview build serves deep links correctly.
cases.reason: Building, bundle inspection, and preview parity are build QA — not app-logic tests, and no confirmed secret is being remediated yet.

cases.prompt: That database URL is confirmed in the shipped JS — get it out of the client, rotate the credentials, and fix the env layout.
cases.reason: Confirmed exposure remediation (relocate server-side, rotate, restructure env) is the shipped hardener; build QA detects and proves.

cases.prompt: Write tests for the settings form validation logic.
cases.reason: App logic/component testing is the Vitest engineer; build QA tests the artifact, not the code's behavior.

cases.prompt: CI builds fail intermittently on the test step with no code changes — figure out why.
cases.reason: Intermittent test-step failures are flake diagnosis; build QA handles deterministic build/bundle verification.
